text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Robert Delacroix
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
AGE: 72 years
SEX: Male
ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [Oncology]
ORDERING SERVICE: Medical Oncology
CLINICAL SERVICE: Interventional Radiology / Medical Oncology
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN ID: SP-[REDACTED]
ACCESSION NUMBER: [REDACTED]

CLINICAL HISTORY:
Patient is a 72-year-old male with known metastatic castration-resistant prostatic adenocarcinoma (Gleason 9 [4+5], Grade Group 5), initially diagnosed with bone and nodal metastases. He has been treated with ADT (Lupron), abi/prednisone, Xtandi, and most recently ola 200 mg BID (dose-reduced from 300 mg BID due to grade 3 anemia). He is germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant carrier with confirmed biallelic tumor loss. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed for disease restaging identified new hypodense liver lesions in segment 5 (1.8 cm) and segment 7 (1.2 cm), not present on prior imaging, with new bilateral pulmonary nodules. CT-guided biopsy of the segment 5 hepatic lesion was performed to confirm the nature of this new visceral involvement and to evaluate for histologic transformation.

PROCEDURE TYPE:
CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY, LIVER, SEGMENT 5

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE REDACTED - TO BE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]

RADIOLOGIC GUIDANCE: CT fluoroscopy
NEEDLE GAUGE: 18-gauge coaxial core biopsy needle
PERFORMED BY: Interventional Radiology

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:

A: Liver, segment 5, CT-guided core needle biopsy x3 cores — labeled "liver biopsy core A"
B: Liver, segment 5, CT-guided core needle biopsy (additional passes) — labeled "liver biopsy core B"

Note: A total of three core needle biopsy passes were submitted and processed. All cores evaluated in toto.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Received in formalin, three cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments measuring 0.7 cm, 1.0 cm, and 0.9 cm in length, each approximately 1-2 mm in diameter. Tissue is firm to rubbery, tan-white to pale yellow in color. One fragment displays a grossly nodular area of pale tan tissue distinct from the surrounding hepatic parenchyma. All three cores are submitted entirely in a single cassette for histologic evaluation (cassette A1). Representative material reserved in OCT for potential molecular studies; FFPE block prepared for IHC.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

The hematoxylin and eosin (H&E)-stained sections reveal three core biopsy fragments with partial preservation of normal hepatic architecture in a minority of the tissue. The majority of all three cores are replaced by an infiltrating carcinomatous process.

The neoplastic cells are arranged in a variety of architectural patterns, most prominently small to medium-sized acini, glandular tubules, and focal cribriform structures. The acinar structures are irregular, with luminal spaces occasionally containing pink amorphous secretions. Fused glands and poorly formed glandular units are present. Solid nests are identified focally, comprising approximately 10% of the tumor volume.

The individual neoplastic cells are large, with abundant pale-to-eosinophilic cytoplasm. Nuclei are round to oval with prominent, large, centrally placed nucleoli — some reaching macronucleolar proportions. Nuclear atypia is high-grade. Mitotic figures are identified, estimated at 8-10 per 10 high-power fields. No atypical mitoses identified.

No neuroendocrine morphology is appreciated: no salt-and-pepper chromatin, no rosette formation, no organoid nesting, and no trabecular architecture suggestive of small cell or large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma.

The background hepatic parenchyma (present at one edge of a core fragment) shows mild sinusoidal dilatation and minor lobular architectural distortion, without significant inflammation, fibrosis, or bile duct abnormality. No steatosis or significant hepatocyte ballooning noted.

Perivascular invasion: Suspicious foci present (definitive assessment limited by core biopsy sampling).
Lymphatic invasion: Not identified.
Necrosis: Present, focal (approximately 5% of tumor area).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDIES:

A panel of immunohistochemical stains was performed on 4-micron formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) sections. All stains performed with appropriate positive and negative controls.

  Prostate lineage markers:
    PSA (polyclonal):           POSITIVE — strong, diffuse cytoplasmic and luminal staining in >90% of tumor cells
    PSMA (clone D2-40 clone 3E6): POSITIVE — strong membranous staining, diffuse
    NKX3.1:                     POSITIVE — strong nuclear staining, diffuse (>90% of tumor nuclei)
    Prostein (P501S):            POSITIVE — granular cytoplasmic staining, diffuse
    AR (androgen receptor):     POSITIVE — strong nuclear staining, diffuse (~85% of tumor nuclei)

  Cytokeratin markers:
    CK7:                        NEGATIVE
    CK20:                       NEGATIVE
    CK5/6:                      NEGATIVE
    AE1/AE3 (pan-cytokeratin):  POSITIVE — moderate, diffuse (consistent with epithelial origin)

  Markers to exclude other primaries:
    CDX2:                       NEGATIVE (excludes colorectal primary)
    TTF-1:                      NEGATIVE (excludes pulmonary adenocarcinoma/thyroid primary)
    PAX8:                       NEGATIVE (excludes renal cell carcinoma, thyroid, gynecologic)
    GATA3:                      NEGATIVE (excludes urothelial and breast primaries)
    Napsin A:                   NEGATIVE
    HepPar-1:                   NEGATIVE (excludes hepatocellular carcinoma)
    Arginase-1:                 NEGATIVE
    GPC-3:                      NEGATIVE

  Neuroendocrine markers (to exclude transdifferentiation):
    Synaptophysin:              NEGATIVE
    Chromogranin A:             NEGATIVE
    CD56 (NCAM):                NEGATIVE
    INSM1:                      NEGATIVE
    Somatostatin receptor 2A:   NEGATIVE

  Proliferation marker:
    Ki-67 (MIB-1):              POSITIVE — approximately 45% of tumor cell nuclei labeled (high proliferative index)

  DNA damage response:
    PTEN (clone 138G6):         NEGATIVE / LOST — complete absence of PTEN expression in tumor cells; positive internal control (stromal and endothelial cells retain staining), consistent with biallelic PTEN deletion

INTERPRETATION OF IHC:
The immunoprofile (PSA+, PSMA+, NKX3.1+, P501S+, AR+, CK7-, CK20-, TTF-1-, CDX2-, GATA3-, synaptophysin-, chromogranin-) is diagnostic of prostatic adenocarcinoma origin. Neuroendocrine transdifferentiation is not supported morphologically or immunophenotypically. PTEN loss by IHC is consistent with biallelic PTEN deletion identified on prior genomic studies.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

In situ hybridization:
  ERG FISH:                     Not performed (ERG-negative on prior primary biopsy)

Molecular testing:
  Additional FFPE tissue block reserved and submitted for comprehensive genomic profiling (see separate NGS report). Results pending at time of this report.

PD-L1 IHC:
  PD-L1 (clone 22C3):          CPS < 5 (estimated CPS approximately 1-2); low PD-L1 expression

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:

LIVER, SEGMENT 5, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

   METASTATIC PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA

   - Consistent with high-grade acinar adenocarcinoma, morphologically aligned with
     previously sampled primary prostatic adenocarcinoma (Gleason 4+5=9, Grade Group 5)
   - No evidence of neuroendocrine transdifferentiation
   - IHC profile confirms prostatic origin
   - PTEN loss confirmed by IHC (consistent with biallelic PTEN deletion)
   - Ki-67 proliferative index: approximately 45% (high)
   - Androgen receptor expression retained (strong nuclear, ~85% of cells)
   - PSA and PSMA strongly positive

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:

The morphology and immunophenotype of this hepatic core biopsy are diagnostic of metastatic prostatic adenocarcinoma. The acinar architecture with prominent nucleoli and high-grade cytology is consistent with the patient's known Gleason 9 (Grade Group 5) primary prostate cancer. No histologic evidence of small cell or large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma transformation is present on this specimen, which is a clinically important distinction given the patient's prior treatment with multiple lines of androgen receptor pathway inhibitors.

Androgen receptor expression is retained and strong, though AR splice variant activity (e.g., AR-V7) cannot be assessed by standard IHC on this specimen; this is best evaluated by dedicated molecular assay.

The high Ki-67 labeling index (~45%) is consistent with aggressive tumor biology in the setting of treatment-refractory mCRPC.

PTEN loss by IHC correlates with biallelic deletion previously identified on genomic profiling of the primary tumor and is consistent with the known aggressive clinical phenotype.

Correlation with clinical history, prior pathology (SP-[REDACTED], transrectal biopsy), and updated molecular profiling is recommended.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
PATHOLOGY RESIDENT/FELLOW: [REDACTED]

ELECTRONIC SIGNATURE: Verified and finalized
REPORT STATUS: FINAL

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DISTRIBUTION: Medical Oncology (Attending), Interventional Radiology (Procedure), Electronic Medical Record
